title: veronica laeta subsp. arenosa
intro: Veronica laeta subsp. arenosa, also known as the Mountain Speedwell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the plantain family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Veronica laeta subsp. arenosa include its ability to soothe sore throats and reduce fever, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
